Zusanli (ST 36) is the He-Sea point of the Stomach Meridian of Foot Yangming. Because of the interior-exterior relationship between the Spleen and Stomach Meridians, Zusanli (ST 36) is chosen to dispel dampness by improving the function of the spleen in transportation and transformation. Heat will disappear when dampness is dispelled. The above points combined together are suitable for the treatment of impotence caused by damp heat.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Impotence is referred to as lack of copulative power in males.","Patterns":"➣ Impotence is generally due to indulgence in sex or excessive masturbation, which makes Mingmen fire decline and exhausts the kidney essence. It may also be due to fear, fright or worry, which damages the qi of the heart, spleen and kidney. Just as said in","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"[b]➣ a) Decline of Mingmen fire[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Failure of the penis in erection, or weak erection, pallor, cold extremities, dizziness, listlessness, soreness and weakness of the lumbar area and knees, frequent urination, pale tongue with white coating, deep thready pulse. If the heart and spleen qi is damaged, palpitations and insomnia may be present.

[b]➣ b) Downward flowing of damp heat[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Inability of the penis to erect, complicated with bitter taste in the mouth, thirst, hot and dark red urine, soreness and weakness of the lower extremities, yellow, sticky tongue coating, soft, rapid pulse.","Treatment Principle":"[b]➣ a) Decline of Mingmen fire[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Conception Vessel and Kidney Meridian are selected as the principal points. Reinforcing method with moxibus- tion is applied to invigorate the kidney yang

[b]➣ b) Downward flowing of damp heat[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Conception Vessel and Spleen Meridian are selected as the principal points. Reducing method is applied to eliminate the damp heat. [b]Prescription:[/b] Zhongji (CV 3), Sanyinjiao (SP 6), Yinlingquan (SP 9), Zusanli (ST 36).","Point Prescription":"[b]➣ a) Decline of Mingmen fire[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Guanyuan (CV 4), Mingmen (GV 4), Shenshu (BL 23), Taixi (KI 3).

➣ [b]➣ b) Downward flowing of damp heat[/b]","Supplementary Points":"[b]➣ a) Decline of Mingmen fire[/b]

➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b]

➣ [b]For damage of the qi of the heart and spleen:[/b] Xinshu (BL 15), Shenmen (HT 7), Sanyinjiao (SP 6).

➣ [b]➣ b) Downward flowing of damp heat[/b]","Remarks":"➣ Impotence in most cases is a functional disorder, for instance, sexual neurasthenia.

➣ [b]Appendix:[/b] Seminal Emission

➣ [b]Seminal emission may be divided into two types:[/b] nocturnal emission and spermatorrhea. Generally, in adult males, unmarried or married, occasional emission is not pathological.

[b]➣ a) Nocturnal emission[/b]

➣ Nocturnal emission is mainly due to over- contemplation or excessive sexual activities, which lead to disharmony between the heart and kidney. If the heart fire fails to descend and control the kidney water, the kidney water cannot ascend and cool the heart fire. When water deficiency and fire excess disturb the essence, nocturnal emission happens in dreams. Moreover, there are dizziness, palpitation, listlessness, lassitude and scanty yellow urine, red tongue, and thready, rapid pulse.","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Impotence","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Insomnia","Full Name":"Appendix Poor Memory | StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"[b]➣ a) Deficiency of both the heart and spleen gi[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] When there is impairment of the heart and spleen, blood is insufficient to house the mind, so dream-disturbed sleep, poor memory and palpitation occur. Sallow complexion and pale tongue are manifestations of a poor blood supply which is unable to nourish the upper part of the body. Dysfunction of the spleen and stomach in transportation and transformation causes anorexia. Deficiency of qi and blood leads to qi declining and blood shortage, resulting in lassitude, listlessness, thready and weak pulse.

[b]➣ b) Disharmony between the heart and kidney[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Restlessness, poor memory, palpitation, nocturnal emission and lower back pain are due to deficiency of the kidney and hyperactivity of the heart fire. Dry mouth with little saliva, burning sensation of the chest, palms and soles, red tongue and thready, rapid pulse are the signs of yin deficiency in the lower energizer with fire flaring up. Dizziness and tinni- tus result from the flaring up of the ministerial fire due to deficiency of the kidney yin.

[b]➣ c) Upward disturbance of the liver fire[/b]

➣ [b]Analyses:[/b] The liver fire flares up to disturb the mind, causing dream-disturbed sleep, fright and fear. When the flaring fire of the liver attacks the head, headache occurs. The long- term liver qi stagnation is transformed into fire, leading to irritability. The liver fire flares bitter taste in the mouth. When the fire stag- up with upward flow of the bile, producing nates in the Liver Meridian, there is distending pain in the costal region. The string-taut pulse is a sign of hyperactivity of the liver.

[b]➣ d) Dysfunction of the stomach[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] With the dysfunction of the spleen and stomach in transportation and transformation, the food accumulates in the middle energizer, obstructing passage, and giving rise to suffocating feeling and distending pain in the epigastric region and difficulty in defecation. Therefore, sleep is disturbed. The undigested food staying in the middle energizer forms dampness and produces phlegm, so the tongue coating is sticky and the pulse rolling.","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Shenmen (HT 7), the Yuan Primary point of the Heart Meridian, calms the heart and soothes the mind. Sanyinjiao (SP 6), the crossing point of the Liver, Spleen and Kidney Meridians, regulates the three meridians. Anmian (Extra) is an extra point for insomnia. Xinshu (BL 15), the Back-Shu point of the heart, and Pishu (BL 20), the Back-Shu point of the spleen, are combined to reinforce the spleen and nourish the heart. Yinbai (SP 1), the Jing-Well point of the Spleen Meridian, is effective for dream-disturbed sleep. Xinshu (BL 15) reduces heart fire. Shenshu (BL 23) and Taixi (KI 3) reinforce kidney water. The combination of these points is to harmonize the heart and kidney. The combination of Ganshu (BL 18), Danshu (BL 19) and Wangu (GB 12) is to subdue the fire of the liver and gallbladder. The combination of Weishu (BL 21) and Zusanli (ST 36) is to regulate the stomach and soothe the mind.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ [b]Insomnia has different patterns:[/b] difficulty in falling asleep after retiring, early awakening, intermittent waking through the period of attempted sleep, and even inability to sleep all night.

➣ Insomnia is often accompanied by dizziness, headache, palpitation, poor memory and mental disorders.","Patterns":"➣[b] a) Anxiety and overwork damage the heart and spleen. Blood is exhausted and the mind is disturbed in case of damage of the heart, while qi and blood production becomes poor in case of deficiency of the spleen qi. Blood deficiency is unable to nourish the heart, leading to insomnia. Just as Zhang Jingyue described, "Overwork and anxiety cause ex- haustion of blood and disturb the mind. As a result, insomnia follows."[/b]

➣ Congenital deficiency, indulgent sexual activity, or a prolonged illness damages the kidney yin. The kidney water fails to ascend smoothly to the heart to check the heart fire, and the heart yang is therefore hyperative. A violent emotional fit can induce flaring of the heart fire which fails to descend to the kidney to control the kidney water. The kidney yin is therefore deficient. Deficiency of kidney yin injures the will and excess of the heart fire dis- turbs the mind. In either case there is a disharmony between the heart and the kidney, and hence insomnia.

➣ Emotional depression causes stagnation of qi in the liver. The long-term stagnant qi of is transformed into fire, which flares up to disturb the mind, and then insomnia occurs.

➣ Irregular food intake damages the spleen and stomach. The accumulated undigested food produces phlegm heat in the middle energizer, which in turn causes dysfunction of the stomach and insomnia, as stated in Internal Classic that sleep is disturbed if the function of the stomach is in disharmony.

➣ In summary, insomnia is related to dysfunction of the heart, spleen, liver and kidney, although there are many other causative factors. Blood is made from food essence and supplies the heart with nourishment. Blood is stored in the liver, and the liver is soothed by the blood. Blood is controlled by the spleen, where production of essence from blood continues. The essence is stored in the kidney. When the kidney essence ascends to the heart and the heart qi descends to the kidney, the heart and kidney are harmonious and the mind is at ease. Whenever there is anxiety, depression, or overwork to damage the heart, spleen, liver or kidney, essence and blood are consumed and mutually affected, resulting in insomnia.","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"[b]➣ a) Deficiency of both the heart and spleen gi[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Difficulty in falling asleep, dream-disturbed sleep, palpitation, poor memory, lassitude, listlessness, anorexia, sallow complexion, pale tongue with a thin coating, thready, weak pulse.

[b]➣ b) Disharmony between the heart and kidney[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Restlessness, insomnia, dizziness, tinnitus, dry mouth with little saliva, burning sensation of the chest, palms and soles, red tongue, thready, rapid pulse, or nocturnal emission, poor memory, palpitation, lower back pain.

[b]➣ c) Upward disturbance of the liver fire[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Irritability, dream-disturbed sleep, fright and fear accompanied with headache, distending pain in the costal region, bitter taste in the mouth and string-taut pulse.

➣ [b]Analyses:[/b] The liver fire flares up to disturb the mind, causing dream-disturbed sleep, fright and fear. When the flaring fire of the liver attacks the head, headache occurs. The long- term liver qi stagnation is transformed into fire, leading to irritability. The liver fire flares bitter taste in the mouth. When the fire stag- up with upward flow of the bile, producing nates in the Liver Meridian, there is distending pain in the costal region. The string-taut pulse is a sign of hyperactivity of the liver.

[b]➣ d) Dysfunction of the stomach[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Insomnia, suffocating feeling and distending pain in the epigastric region, belching, or difficult defecation, sticky tongue coating, and rolling pulse.","Treatment Principle":"➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Heart Meridian are selected as the main points to calm the heart and soothe the mind. [b]Deficiency of the heart and spleen:[/b] Reinforcing method with moxibustion in combination is applied to strengthen the heart and spleen. [b]Disharmony between the heart and kidney:[/b] Even movement is applied to harmonize the heart and kidney. [b]Upward disturbance of the liver fire:[/b] Reducing is applied to subdue the liver fire. [b]Dysfunction of stomach:[/b] Reducing is applied to regulate the stomach qi.","Point Prescription":"➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Shenmen (HT 7), Sanyinjiao (SP 6), Anmian (Extra).","Supplementary Points":"➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b]

➣ [b]Deficiency of the heart and spleen:[/b] Pishu (BL 20), Xinshu (BL 15), Yinbai (SP 1, moxi- bustion with small moxa cones).

➣ [b]Disharmony between the heart and kidney:[/b] Xinshu (BL 15), Shenshu (BL 23), Taixi (KI 3).

➣ [b]Upward disturbance of the liver fire:[/b] Gans- hu (BL 18), Danshu (BL 19), Wangu(GB 12).

➣ [b]Dysfunction of the stomach:[/b] Weishu (BL 21), Zusanli (ST 36).","Remarks":"➣ [b]Dermal needle therapy:[/b] Tap Sishenchong (Extra), Back-Shu points or Huatuojiaji (Extra) slightly from above downward two to three times. Treat once daily or every other day. Ten treatments constitute a course. Next course of treatment begins after an interval of two to three days.

➣ [b]Appendix:[/b] Poor Memory

➣ Poor memory is characterized by the functional decline of the brain, hypomnesia and forgetfulness. It differs from lack of intel- ligence and natural endowments. The condition in most cases is caused by insufficiency of the heart and spleen and deficiency of the kidney essence. As Wang Yang said, "Essence and will are both stored in the kidney. If the kidney essence is deficient, the will is weakened. Poor memory appears when the will fails to cooperate with the heart." [b]In Prescriptions Based on Three Pathogenic Factors, it says:[/b] "The spleen dominates recollection and thinking. Recollection refers to the power of recalling past facts to the mind, and thinking depends on the action of the heart.... Since the spleen is troubled, collection is impaired, and the mind is uneasy, so memory is poor." The heart and spleen dominate the blood. Overthinking injuring the heart and spleen consumes blood and leads to poor memory. The kidney dominates essence and marrow, which can be consumed or exhausted by indulgent sexual activity. The brain is therefore poorly nourished, causing forgetfulness. The aged also tend to have poor memory due to the kidney decline.

➣ The treatment is mainly to replenish the blood of the heart and reinforce the spleen and kidney. Reinforcing method is applied to Sishencong (Extra), Xinshu (BL 15), Pishu (BL 20), Zusanli (ST 36), Shenshu (BL 23), and Zhaohai (KI 6).

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Sishencong (Extra) is an empirical point for treatment of poor memory. Xinshu (BL 15) and Pishu (BL 20) are applied to strengthen the heart and spleen. Shenshu (BL 23) and Zhaohai (KI 6) promote the kidney essence, produce marrow and replenish the brain. Zusanli (ST 36) reinforces the spleen and stomach in transportation and transformation, and replenishes qi and blood.","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Insomnia","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Palpitation","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] The combination of Shenmen (HT 7), the Yuan-Primary point of the Heart Meridian, and Xinshu (BL 15) with Juque (CV 14), the Front-Mu point of the heart, and Neiguan (PC 6), the Luo-Connecting point of the Pericardium Meridian can regulate qi and blood of the heart to ease the mind. The combination of Tongli (HT 5), the Luo- Connecting point of the Heart Meridian, and Qiuxu (GB 40), the Yuan-Primary point of the Gallbladder Meridian, can calm the mind and regulate the gallbladder. Fenglong (ST 40), the Luo-Connecting point of the Stomach Meridian, and Danshu (BL 19), the Back-Shu point of the gallbladder, can dissolve phlegm and dispel heat. Pishu (BL 20) and Weishu (BL 21) can regulate the spleen and stomach to promote qi and blood production. Zusanli (ST 36) is an important point to reinforce qi and blood. Shenshu (BL 23) and Taixi (KI 3) can replenish kidney yin. Jueyinshu (BL 14) can clear heart fire. Guanyuan (CV 14), Shuifen (CV 9) and Yinlingquan (SP 9) can invigorate the heart yang, strengthen the spleen and remove the harmful fluid.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Palpitation refers to unduly rapid action of the heart which is felt by the patient and accompanied by nervousness and restlessness.

➣ Mild palpitation is mostly due to sudden fright and overstrain. The general condition is comparatively good and the symptoms are of short duration. A serious case is often due to

➣ prolonged internal injury. The general condition is comparatively poor and the symptoms are severe.","Patterns":"➣[b] a) Disturbance of the mind: A timid person is likely to have palpitation when he or she is frightened by strange noises, surprising objects, or dangerous environments. In the nineteenth chapter of Plain Questions, it says: "Fright makes qi disturbed because the heart has nothing to rely on, the mind has no place to house and the thinking has nothing to focus on." There are other pathogenic factors causing palpitation, such as internal accumulation of phlegm heat, mental depression and anger, dysfunction of the stomach and upward perversion of phlegm fire.[/b]

➣[b] b) Insufficiency of qi and blood: Persistent disease, weak constitution, loss of blood, or b) Insufficiency of qi and blood: Persistent overthinking damages the heart and spleen, and impedes the production of qi and blood. Deficiency of qi and blood fails to nourish the heart, which affects the housing of the mind, causing palpitation.[/b]

➣[b] c) Hyperactivity of the fire due to yin deficiency: Injury of kidney yin by indulgent sexual activity, or debility after a prolonged disease renders the kidney water unable to check the heart fire. Disharmony between the heart and kidney with flaring fire disturbing the mind causes palpitation.[/b]

➣[b] d) Retention of harmful fluid: Retention of harmful fluid due to depression of the heart yang or due to deficiency of the spleen and kidney yang disturbs the heart, resulting in palpitation.[/b]","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"","Treatment Principle":"➣ [b]Method:[/b] The Back-Shu and Front-Mu points of the heart, and points of the Heart and Pericardium Meridians are selected as the main points. Even-movement method is applied for disturbance of the mind to calm the heart. Reinforcing method is used for insufficiency of qi and blood to nourish the heart and ease the mind. Reinforcing combined with reducing method is applied for fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency to nourish yin and subdue the fire. For retention of harmful fluid, reducing method is applied first and then reinforcing in combination with moxibustion to warm yang and dissolve the harmful fluid. [b]Prescription:[/b] Xinshu (BL 15), Juque (CV 14), Shenmen (HT 7), Neiguan (PC 6).","Point Prescription":"","Supplementary Points":"➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b]

➣ [b]Disturbance of the mind:[/b] Tongli (PC 5), Qinxu (GB 40);

➣ [b]if accompanied with phlegm heat:[/b] Fenglong (ST 40), Danshu (BL 19).

➣ [b]Insufficiency of qi and blood:[/b] Pishu (BL 20), Weishu (BL. 21), Zusanli (ST 36).

➣ [b]Fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency:[/b] Jueyinshu (BL. 14), Shenshu (BL 23), Taixi (KI 3).

➣ [b]Retention of harmful fluid:[/b] Shuifen (CV 9), Guanyuan (CV 4), Shenque (CV 8), Yin- lingquan (SP 9).","Remarks":"➣ Palpitation described here may be involved in neurosis, functional disorders of the auto- nomic nervous system and cardiac arrhythmia of various origins.","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Palpitation","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Manic Depressive Disorder","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"[b]➣ a) Depressive disorder[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] This condition is caused by the stagnation of phlegm and qi, which injures the heart, liver and spleen. Xinshu (BL 15) is used to clear the heart, Ganshu (BL 18) to remove the liver stagnation, Pishu (BL 20) to promote the spleen qi circulation, Shenmen (HT 7) and Fenglong (ST 40) are used to dissolve the phlegm for calming the mind.

[b]➣ b) Manic disorder[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Dazhui (GV 14) and Shuigou (GV 26) are used to reduce heat for clearing the mind. Fengfu (GV 16) [b]is selected for mental disorders as Miraculous Pivot states:[/b] "The brain is the sea of marrow, its upper part reaches the vertex of the cranium, and its lower part reaches point Fengfu (GV 16)." Neiguan (PC 6) is combined with Fenglong (ST 40) to clear the heart and dissolve the phlegm.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Depressive disorder is manifested by mental dejection, reticence or incoherent speech, while manic disorder by shouting, restlessness and violent behaviors. As described in Classic on Medical Problems, depressive disorder is caused by excessive yin, while manic disorder by abundant yang

➣ The most important etiological factor of manic-depressive disorder is emotional injury. Pathogenetically, phlegm plays the primary role. Depressive disorder is due to stagnation of phlegm combined with qi, while manic disorder is due to phlegm fire. Although they are different in symptomatology, they are related to each other. A prolonged depressive disorder, in which fire is produced by phlegm stagnation, may change into manic disorder, while a protracted manic disorder, in which the stagnated fire is gradually dispersed, but the phlegm is still existing, can change into depressive disorder. Therefore, they are termed together as manic-depressive disorder.","Patterns":"","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"","Treatment Principle":"[b]➣ a) Depressive disorder[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Even movement method is applied to the points of the Heart and Liver Meridians to soothe the liver, calm the heart and dissolve the phlegm.

[b]➣ b) Manic disorder[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Reducing method is applied to the main points of the Governor Vessel and Pericardium Meridian of Hand Jueyin to calm the heart, ease the mind, reduce the heat and dissolve the phlegm.","Point Prescription":"[b]➣ a) Depressive disorder[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Xinshu (BL 15), Ganshu (BL 18), Pishu (BL 20), Shenmen (HT 7), Fenglong (ST 40).

➣ [b]➣ b) Manic disorder[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Dazhui(GV 14), Fengfu (GV 16), Shuigou (GV 26), Neiguan (PC 6), Feng- long (ST 40).","Supplementary Points":"[b]➣ a) Depressive disorder[/b]

➣ [b]➣ b) Manic disorder[/b]

➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b]

➣ [b]Mania with extreme heat:[/b] Prick the twelve Jing-Well points on the hand (LU 11, HT 9, PC 9, LI 1, TE 1, SI 1) to bleed for reducing heat.","Remarks":"[b]➣ a) The condition described here includes the depressive and manic types of schizophrenia in modern medicine.[/b]

[b]➣ b) Thirteen points for manic-depressive disorder: Prick to bleed according to the order of Shuigou (GV 26), Shaoshang (LU 11), Yinbai (SP 1), Daling (PC 7), Shenmai (BL 62), Fengfu (GV 16), Jiache (ST 6), Cheng- jiang (CV 24), Laogong (PC 8), Shangxing (GV 23), Huiyin (CV 1), Quchi (LI 11), and She- xiazhongfeng (an extra point located at the midline of the under side of the tongue).[/b]","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Manic Depressive Disorder","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Epilepsy","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"[b]➣ a) During seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Dizziness, headache and suffocated sensation in the chest are the prodromal symptoms which show the upward perversion of the wind phlegm. The liver wind stirs up with the phlegm to disturb the mind. Therefore, there are loss of consciousness, convulsions and upward staring of the eyes. The foam on the lips is owing to the ascending wind phlegm. White, sticky tongue coating and rolling pulse are signs of retaining of the phlegm. Since the wind phlegm is irregularly accumulated and dispersed, the seizures are paroxysmal, and the patient behaves as normal after the seizure.

[b]➣ b) After seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Listlessness is due to damage of the vital qi by frequent epileptic fits. With insufficiency of blood, the complexion is lus- treless. There is dizziness if the brain lacks blood supply, and palpitation if the heart is poorly supplied with blood. Due to the depression of the spleen yang, the food cannot be transformed into essence, and dampness and phlegm are produced, so there are anorexia and profuse sputum. Deficiency of the kidney essence causes soreness and weakness of the lower back and limbs. Pale tongue with white coating and thready, rolling pulse suggest consumption of qi and blood and accumulation of phlegm dampness.","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"[b]➣ a) During seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Shuigou (GV 26) and Jiuwei (CV 15) are used for resuscitation. Jianshi (PC 5), Fenglong (ST 40) and Taicong (LR 3) are used to calm the heart, ease the mind, dissolve the phlegm and dispel the wind.

[b]➣ b) After seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Xinshu (BL 15), Yintang (GV 29) and Shenmen (HT 7) are used for nourishing the heart and easing the mind, and Sanyin- jiao (SP 6) and Taixi (KI 3), for strengthening the spleen and reinforcing the kidney. Yaoqi (Extra) is an empirical point for epilepsy. Shen- mai (BL 62), a point of Yang Heel Vessel, is needled for daytime seizure, while Zhao-hai (KI 6), a point of Yin Heel Vessel, is needled for night seizure. Zhongwan (CV 12) and Fen- glong (ST 40) are applied to regulate the stomach and dissolve the phlegm. Guanyuan (CV 4) and Zusanli (ST 36) are used to regulate and replenish qi and blood.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Epilepsy is manifested as seizures, falling down in a fit, loss of consciousness, foam on the lips, or screams with eyes staring upward, and convulsions. After some minutes, consciousness returns, and the patient's condition becomes normal.

➣ Besides the typical seizures, there may be variations. It can be a momentary loss of attention or consciousness with eyes staring directly forward, or prolonged loss of consciousness associated with convulsions and foam on the lips. Epileptic fits may occur at any time, in various frequency and with different severity. It is often preceded by an "aura" of dizziness, depression sensation of the chest, and listlessness. Generally speaking, epilepsy is an excess condition, but frequent recurrence can lower the body's resistance.","Patterns":"➣[b] a) Fear and fright: Fear makes qi disordered and fright makes qi descend, affecting the liver and kidney and leading to stirring of the deficiency wind.[/b]

➣[b] b) Dysfunction of the liver in smoothing the flow of qi, or irregular food intake damaging the spleen and stomach, makes the dampness of food and drinks accumulate as phlegm, which combined with the stagnated liver qi, disturbs the mind and causes epilepsy.[/b]

➣[b] c) Epilepsy may result from hereditary factors, but in most of the hereditary cases it comes on in early childhood.[/b]","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"[b]➣ a) During seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] A typical seizure is preceded by dizziness, headache and suffocating sensation in the chest, and immediately followed by falling down with loss of consciousness, pallor, clenched jaws, upward staring of the eyes, convulsion, foam on the lips, screaming like pigs or sheep, and even incontinence of urine and feces. Gradually, the patient regains consciousness, and the symptoms disappear. Apart from fatigue and weakness, the patient can live a normal life. White, sticky tongue coating, and string-taut, rolling pulse are present.

[b]➣ b) After seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Listlessness, lustreless complexion, dizziness, palpitation, anorexia, profuse sputum, weakness and soreness of the lower back and limbs, pale tongue with white coating and thready, rolling pulse.","Treatment Principle":"[b]➣ a) During seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Governor and Conception Vessels and Liver Meridian are selected as the main points with reducing method to dissolve the phlegm, induce resuscitation, soothe the liver and dispel the wind.

[b]➣ b) After seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Heart, Spleen and Kidney Meridians are selected as the main points with even movement to nourish the heart, ease the mind, strengthen the spleen and reinforce the kidney.","Point Prescription":"[b]➣ a) During seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Shuigou (GV 26), Jiuwei (CV 15), Jianshi (PC 5), Taichong (LR 3), Fenglong (ST 40).

➣ [b]➣ b) After seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Xinshu (BL 15), Yintang (GV 29), Shenmen (HT 7), Sanyinjiao (SP 6), Taixi (KI 3), Yaoqi (Extra).","Supplementary Points":"[b]➣ a) During seizure[/b]

➣ [b]➣ b) After seizure[/b]

➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b]

➣ [b]Daytime seizure:[/b] Shenmai (BL 62).

➣ [b]Night seizure:[/b] Zhaohai (KI 6).

➣ [b]Phlegm stagnation:[/b] Zhongwan (CV 12), Fenglong (ST 40).

➣ [b]Severe deficiency of qi and blood:[/b] Guanyuan (CV 4), Zusanli (ST 36).","Remarks":"➣ The above description refers to many types of epileptic seizures including grand mal, petit mal, psychomotor and focal seizures. For secondary epilepsy, the primary disease should be treated actively.","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Epilepsy","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Dizziness ","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"[b]➣ a) Hyperactivity of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Anger damages the liver yin, causing hyperactivity of the liver yang, which transforms into fire. When the fire flares up, flushed face, red eyes and irritability appear. The spirit stored in the liver is upset by disorders of the liver, then dream-disturbed sleep occurs. The taste in the mouth, string-taut, rapid pulse are red tongue proper with yellow coating, bitter the signs of yin deficiency resulting in fire hy- peractivity.

[b]➣ b) Deficiency of gi and blood[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Dizziness is inevitable because deficiency of qi and blood fails to nourish the brain. The heart dominates the blood and is manifested in the complexion. The spleen dominates transportation and transformation to manufacture qi and blood. If the heart and spleen are injured, qi and blood will be insufficient, thereby, the complexion is lustreless, and the lips and the nails are pale. Deficiency of blood leads to palpitation and insomnia. Deficiency of qi gives rise to weakness, lassitude, anorexia, and is aggravated by overwork. Pale tongue and thready, weak pulse are signs of deficiency of qi and blood.

[b]➣ c) Interior retention of phlegm dampness[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Dizziness with a heavy feeling of the head is the sign of the pure yang disturbed by phlegm dampness. Suffocating sensation in the chest and nausea are caused by qi obstructed in the middle energizer. Anorexia and somnolence are due to the spleen yang deficiency. White, sticky tongue coating, soft and rolling pulse are the signs of phlegm dampness.","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"[b]➣ a) Hyperactivity of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] The reinforcing method applied to Shenshu (BL 23) and Taixi (KI 3) is to replenish the kidney water, while the reducing method to Ganshu (BL 18), Xingjian (LR 2) and Fengchi (GB 20) is to pacify the liver yang.

[b]➣ b) Deficiency of qi and blood[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Moxibustion to Baihui (GV 20), which is located at the vertex, is to make qi and blood ascend to the head to nourish the brain and reduce dizziness. Guanyuan (CV 4) is used to strengthen the primary qi. Pishu (BL 20) and Sanyinjiao (SP 6) are for invigorating the spleen and stomach to produce qi and blood.

[b]➣ c) Interior retention of phlegm dampness[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Pishu (BL 20) and Zhongwan (CV 12) are needled to strengthen the spleen and stomach for eliminating dampness. Feng- long (ST 40), the Luo-Connecting point of the stomach, is to make qi descend and resolve phlegm. Touwei (ST 8) is for dizziness. Nei- guan (PC 6) is for relaxing the chest, regulating qi and harmonizing the stomach to stop vom- iting.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Mild cases can be relieved by closing one's eyes, while serious cases have an illusion of bodily movement with rotatory sensation like sitting in a sailing boat or moving car, and may be accompanied by nausea, vomiting and sweating,","Patterns":"➣[b] a) Hyperactivity of the liver yang[/b]

➣ The liver is analogized as wind and wood, characterized by movement and ascending. Overcontemplation, anxiety, depression or anger can damage the liver yin, resulting in hyperactivity of liver yang. Dizziness occurs when the liver yang moves as the wind and ascends to attack the brain or the kidney water, generally in deficiency, fails to nourish the liver. Dizziness occurs when the liver is not nourished, which leads to hyperactivity of liver yang. In both situations there is deficiency in the lower but excess in the upper part of the body.

➣[b] b) Deficiency of qi and blood[/b]

➣ The heart and spleen are damaged by overwork and over contemplation, or a weak constitution after a disease. The damaged spleen fails to produce qi and blood, leading to deficiency. In case the brain is poorly nourished by qi and blood, dizziness occurs.

➣[b] c) Interior retention of phlegm dampness[/b]

➣ In a person with generally abundant phlegm dampness, irregular food intake and overwork damage the stomach and the spleen, impairing their function in transportation and transformation and leading to production of dampness and phlegm. Then the stagnant phlegm and qi may impede the ascending of clear yang and the descending of turbid yin, and thus dizziness occurs.","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"[b]➣ a) Hyperactivity of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Dizziness aggravated by anger, irritability, flushed face, red eyes, tinnitus, bitter taste in the mouth, dream-disturbed sleep, red tongue with yellow coating, string-taut, rapid pulse.

[b]➣ b) Deficiency of gi and blood[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Dizziness accompanied by pallor and lustreless complexion, weakness, palpitation, insomnia, pale lips and nails, lassitude, pale tongue proper, thready and weak pulse. Dizziness occurs mostly after a serious disease or loss of blood and is aggravated by overwork. Loss of consciousness happens in severe cases.

[b]➣ c) Interior retention of phlegm dampness[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Dizziness with a heavy feeling of the head and suffocating sensation in the chest, nausea, profuse sputum, anorexia, somnolence, white, sticky tongue coating, soft, rolling pulse.","Treatment Principle":"[b]➣ a) Hyperactivity of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Liver Meridian and Kidney Meridian are selected as the main points to nourish yin and pacify yang. Reinforcing and reducing methods are applied with either one first according to the condition of the disease.

[b]➣ b) Deficiency of qi and blood[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Conception Vessel and the Bladder and Stomach Meridians are selected as the main points with reinforcing method in combination with moxibustion to replenish qi and blood.

[b]➣ c) Interior retention of phlegm dampness[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] The Back-Shu and Front-Mu points of the spleen and stomach are selected as the main points with even movement method to resolve phlegm and eliminate dampness.","Point Prescription":"[b]➣ a) Hyperactivity of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Fengchi (GB 20), Ganshu (BL 18), Shenshu (BL 23), Taixi (KI 3), Xingjian (LR 2).

➣ [b]➣ b) Deficiency of qi and blood[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Baihui (GV 20), Pishu (BL 20), Guanyuan (CV 4), Zusanli (ST 36), Sanyin- jiao (SP 6).

➣ [b]➣ c) Interior retention of phlegm dampness[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Touwei (ST 8), Pishu (BL 20), Zhongwan (CV 12), Neiguan (PC 6), Fenglong (ST 40).","Supplementary Points":"[b]➣ a) Hyperactivity of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]➣ b) Deficiency of qi and blood[/b]

➣ [b]➣ c) Interior retention of phlegm dampness[/b]","Remarks":"[b]➣ a) Dizziness may be explained as derangement of the equilibrium of the senses in modern medicine. Clinically, the symptom is mostly seen in hypertension, arteriosclerosis, neurosis, and otogenic diseases.[/b]

[b]➣ b) Dermal needle therapy[/b]

➣ [b]Main points:[/b] Baihui (GV 20), Taiyang (Extra), Yintang (GV 29), and Huatuojiaji (Extra).

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Tap once or twice daily with moderate stimulation. Five to ten treatments constitute one course.","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Dizziness ","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Melancholia","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"[b]➣ a) Depression of the gi in the liver[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] In case of emotional injury, the liver fails to be harmonious and flourishing, so mental depression appears. The Liver Meridian of Foot-Jueyin runs up to the lower abdomen and curves around the stomach and then branches out in the costal and hypochondriac regions. In case of stagnation of the qi of the liver, there may appear distress in the chest, hy- pochondriac pain and abdominal distension. If the stomach qi fails to descend, belching and anorexia occur. When the liver qi encroaches on the spleen, abdominal pain, vomiting and abnormal bowel movement result. Thin, sticky tongue coating and string-taut pulse are the signs of disharmony between the liver and stomach.

[b]➣ b) Transformation of depressed qi into fire[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] When depressed qi is transformed into fire, it flares up along the Liver Meridian, resulting in headache, red eyes and tinnitus. When the liver fire evaporates the fluid and heat accumulates in the stomach and intes- tines, dryness and bitter taste in the mouth and constipation occur. If the liver is hyperactive, it will encroach upon the stomach, leading to failure of the stomach's descending function, then distress of the chest, hypochondriac distension, and acid regurgitation occur. Irritabil- ity, yellow tongue coating, string-taut, rapid pulse are the signs of the fire in the liver.

[b]➣ c) Stagnation of phlegm (also known as globus hystericus)[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] The depressed liver qi invades the spleen and stomach, leading to disturbance of transportation and transformation. Dampness derived from the water and food taken is gathered and turned into phlegm, which combined with qi, stays in the throat, giving rise to a choking feeling. Thin, sticky tongue coating and string-taut, rolling pulse are the signs of the stagnation of phlegm with qi.

[b]➣ d) Insufficiency of blood (also known as hysteria)[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Because of overcontemplation and emotional frustration, the qi function is impaired and the blood is gradually consumed, leading to poor nourishment of the mind, thus the above-mentioned symptoms occur. In case the qi is blocked, there may be sudden distress of the chest, hiccup, sudden aphonia and convulsion. Thin, white tongue coating, and string taut, thready pulse are the signs of a long- standing stagnation of qi that damages blood.","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"[b]➣ a) Depression of gi in the liver[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Danzhong (CV 17), the influential point of qi, is able to regulate the flow of qi. Ganshu (GB 18) and Taichong (LR 3) are the Back-Shu point and Yuan-Primary point of the liver respectively. When used in combination, they may soothe the liver and remove depression. Zhongwan (CV 12) and Zu- sanli (ST 36) may harmonize the stomach and make the stomach qi descend. Gongsun (SP 4), the Luo-Connecting point of the Spleen Meridian, may strengthen the spleen and harmonize the stomach.

[b]➣ b) Transformation of depressed qi into fire[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Xingjian (LR 2) and Xiaxi (GB 43), the Ying-Spring points of the Liver and Gallbladder Meridians, may dispel fire from the liver and gallbladder. Zhigou (TE 6) in combination with Yanglingquan (GB 34) may treat distress of the chest, hypochondriac distension, bitter taste in the mouth and constipation. Shangwan (CV 13) may harmonize the stomach and regulate the flow of qi to treat acid regurgitation.

[b]➣ c) Stagnation of phlegm[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Taichong (LR 3) is applied to soothe the liver and remove depression. Tiantu (CV 22) is needled to descend the qi and treat the throat. Neiguan (PC 6) is used to remove depression from the chest and regulate the flow of qi. Danzhong (CV 17), the influential point of qi, Fenglong (ST 40), the Luo-Connecting point of the stomach, used together, may promote the circulation of qi and resolve phlegm.

[b]➣ d) Insufficiency of blood[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Taichong (LR 3) is selected to soothe the liver and remove depression. Juque (CV 14), the Front-Mu point of the Heart Meridian, Shenmen (HT 7), the Yuan-Primary point, combined with Sanyinjiao (SP 6) of the Spleen Meridian, may nourish blood, and refresh and tranquilize the mind. Neiguan (PC 6) and Danzhong (CV 17) may remove the depression of the chest. Gongsun (SP 4) and Tiantu (CV 22) cause the qi to descend and check hiccup. Tongli (HT 5) and Lianquan (CV 23) are effective in the treatment of aphonia. Hegu (LI 4) is taken to regulate the flow of qi. Yanglingquan (GB 34), the influential point of the tendons, is used to stop convulsion and relieve pain. Shuigou (GV 26) and Yongquan (KI 1) are used for resuscitation.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Melancholia is a general term for disorders resulting from emotional depression and stagnation of qi. The symptoms are due to emotional frustration, and depression of qi which lead to stagnation of blood, accumulation of phlegm, retention of food, collection of fire, and disharmony of the zang-fu organs. [b]Zhu Danxi said:[/b] "There is no disease when qi and blood are in harmony. Once depression occurs, disease results."","Patterns":"➣ Generally speaking, melancholia is caused by emotional injuries resulting in disharmony of the activity of the zang organs. [b]As said in the twenty-eighth chapter of the Miraculous Pivot:[/b] "Grief, sorrow, worry and anxiety disturb the mind and disturbance of the mind will affect all the five zang and six fu organs."

➣ Repressed anger may give rise to many disorders of the liver with impairment of the free flow of qi. Then the liver qi may go upward to attack the mind, or conquer the spleen and stomach, or counteract the lung, or go downward to the intestines, leading to various illnesses.

➣ Too much worry may depress the liver and suppress the spleen, causing the failure of the spleen in transportation and transformation, which brings about accumulation of dampness and phlegm and retention of undigested food. accumulation of dampness, phlegm and undi- gested food for a long duration is apt to produce fire. Overanxiety may also lead to dysfunction of the qi and consumption of yin (nutrients and blood), generating many symptoms.","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"[b]➣ a) Depression of the gi in the liver[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Mental depression, distress of the chest, hypochondriac pain, abdominal distension, belching, anorexia, or abdominal pain, vomiting, abnormal bowel movement, thin, sticky tongue coating, string- taut pulse.

[b]➣ b) Transformation of depressed qi into fire[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Headache, dryness and bitter taste in the mouth, irritability, distress of the chest, hypochondriac distension, acid regurgitation, constipation, red eyes, tinni- tus, red tongue with yellow coating, string-taut, rapid pulse.

[b]➣ c) Stagnation of phlegm (also known as globus hystericus)[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Feeling of a lump choking in the throat, hard to spit out or to swallow, thin, sticky tongue coating, string-taut, rolling pulse.

[b]➣ d) Insufficiency of blood (also known as hysteria)[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Grief without reasons, capricious joy or anger, suspicions, tendency to get frightened, palpitation, irritability, insomnia, or sudden distress of the chest, hiccup, sudden aphonia, convulsion, or loss of consciousness in severe cases, thin, white tongue coating, string-taut, thready pulse.","Treatment Principle":"[b]➣ a) Depression of gi in the liver[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] The influential point of qi and the points of the Liver Meridian are selected as the principal points. Even movement method is applied to soothe the liver, strengthen the spleen and harmonize the stomach.

[b]➣ b) Transformation of depressed qi into fire[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Liver, Gallbladder and Stomach Meridians are selected as the principal points. Reducing method is used to dispel fire from the liver and strengthen the stomach function.

[b]➣ c) Stagnation of phlegm[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Liver Meridian and Conception Vessel are selected as the principal points. Even movement method is applied to soothe the liver, remove depression, regulate the flow of qi and resolve phlegm.

[b]➣ d) Insufficiency of blood[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Points of the Heart and Liver Meridians are selected as the principal points. Even movement method is applied to nourish blood, soothe the liver and refresh and tranquilize the mind.","Point Prescription":"[b]➣ a) Depression of gi in the liver[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Ganshu (BL 18), Danzhong (CV 17), Zhongwan (CV 12), Zusanli (ST 36), Gongsun (SP 4), Taichong (LR 3).

[b]➣ b) Transformation of depressed qi into fire[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Shangwan (CV 13), Zhigou (TE 6), Yanglingquan (GB 34), Xingjian (LR 2), Xiaxi (GB 43).

[b]➣ c) Stagnation of phlegm[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Tiantu (CV 22), Danzhong (CV 17), Neiguan (PC 6), Fenglong (ST 40), Taichong (LR 3).

[b]➣ d) Insufficiency of blood[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Juque (CV 14), Shenmen (HT 7), Sanyinjiao (SP 6), Taichong (LR 3).","Supplementary Points":"[b]➣ a) Depression of gi in the liver[/b]

[b]➣ b) Transformation of depressed qi into fire[/b]

[b]➣ c) Stagnation of phlegm[/b]

[b]➣ d) Insufficiency of blood[/b]

➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b] [b]Distress of the chest:[/b] Neiguan (PC 6), Dan- zhong (CV 17). [b]Hiccup:[/b] Gongsun (SP 4), Tiantu (CV 22). [b]Sudden aphonia:[/b] Tongli (HT 5), Lianquan (CV 23). [b]Convulsion:[/b] Hegu (LI 4), Yanglingquan (GB34). [b]Loss of consciousness:[/b] Shuigou (GV 26), Yongquan (KI 1).","Remarks":"➣ This condition is seen in hysteria and neurosis in Western medicine.","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Melancholia","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Xu Lao - Fatigue","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] The spleen qi is damaged, the motor functions of the limbs and muscles decline, so tiredness can not be relieved even by rest. The kidney essence is consumed, the brain has not enough marrow to nourish it, so the memory declines and the patient finds it difficult to concentrate. The heart blood is deficient, leading to headache and insomnia. The disharmony between qi and blood is the cause of poor nourishment of the tendons, muscles and head, producing a sore throat, neck or axillary tenderness, muscle aches, joint pain, headache and insomnia.","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Xinshu (BL 15), Pishu (BL 20), Ganshu (BL 18), Shenshu (BL 23), and Feishu (BL 13) are the Back-Shu points of the five zang organs. Acupuncture with reinforcing method applied to them can regulate and reinforce qi and blood, and the yin and yang of the five zang organs. Danzhong (CV 17) is the influential point of qi, functioning to moisten and nourish the heart and lungs, and regulate qi circulation. Zusanli (ST 36), Qihai (CV 6), and Guanyuan (CV 4) are to reinforce qi and blood. Zhongwan (CV 12) and Dabao (SP 21) are effective for promoting the functions of the spleen and stomach to produce more qi and blood. For deficiency of spleen qi, Taibai (SP 3) and Sanyinjiao (SP 6) are added, because Taibai (SP 3) is the Yuan-Primary point of the Spleen Meridian, and Sanyinjiao (SP 6) is the crossing point of the three Foot Yin meridians. They are used together to reinforce the spleen qi. For insomnia, Shenmen (HT 7) and Zhaohai (KI 6) are added to communicate between the heart and kidneys for harmonizing water and fire. For forgetfulness, Yintang (Extra) and Renzhong (GV 26) are good for strengthening the brain and refreshing the mind. For liver qi stagnation, Taichong (LR 3) and Neiguan (PC 6) are selected for relaxing the chest and regulating qi to relieve emotional stress.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Xu Lao is a syndrome characterized by long-term fatigue accompanied by low fever, headache, pain in the muscles and joints, insomnia, and a variety of mental symptoms, but no abnormal findings in physical examinations or routine laboratory tests. It is caused by long- term overwork or chronic disease, dysfunction of the five zang organs, and the resulting consumption of qi and blood and essence and vitality.","Patterns":"➣ Fatigue is a manifestation of consumption of qi and blood, and essence and vitality, which are produced by the five zang organs. The invasion of exogenous pathogenic fac- tors mostly damages lung qi, which dominates the qi of the whole body. When lung qi is deficient, the symptoms will be tiredness of the whole body, low voice, shortness of breath and weakness of respiration. The liver maintains the free flow of qi. Emotional depression disturbs the liver qi, making it stagnate. The liver controls the tendons and stores blood. When the liver blood is damaged, the tendons will lack nourishment, leading to weakness and numbness of the extremities and motor impairment of the joints. Overthinking consumes the heart blood and qi of the heart and spleen. When the heart qi and blood are deficient, the mind will lack nourishment, causing symptoms such as listlessness, fatigue, insomnia and palpitations. The spleen is the acquired condition after birth. It dominates transportation and transformation of water and food, as well as the muscles and limbs. If the spleen is weak in transportation and transformation, the muscles and limbs will be poorly nourished, producing symptoms including weakness and tiredness. Excessive physical labor or sexual activities consume the kidney essence. The kidneys store essence, dominate the bones and manufactures marrow. When the kidney essence is insufficient, the bones will be weak and the person will look dispirited. Disturbance of qi and blood and the yin and yang of the five zang organs due to various reasons is the pathogenesis of Fatigue.","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Persistent or recur- rent severe fatigue for at least half a year, not relieved by rest, poor memory, difficulty in concentration, sore throat, neck or axillary tenderness, muscle aches, joint pain, headache, insomnia.","Treatment Principle":"➣ [b]Method:[/b] Back-Shu points, points of the Ren-Conception Vessel and Spleen and Stomach Meridians are selected as the main ones. Reinforcing method is applied to replenish qi and blood and harmonize the five zang organs.","Point Prescription":"➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Xinshu (BL 15), Pishu (BL 20), Ganshu (BL 18), Shenshu (BL 23), Feishu (BL 13), Zhongwan (CV 12), Qihai (CV 6), Zusanli (ST 36), Dabao (SP 21)","Supplementary Points":"➣ Supplementary points:[b]vDeficiency of spleen qi:[/b] Taibai (SP 3), Sanyinjiao (SP 6).

➣ [b]Insomnia:[/b] Shenmen (HT 7), Zhaohai (KI 6).

➣ [b]Forgetfulness:[/b] Yintang (Extra), Renzhong (GV 26).

➣ [b]Liver qi stagnation:[/b] Taichong (LR 3), Nei- guan (PC 6).","Remarks":"➣ 1. This disease can be seen in chronic fatigue syndrome.

➣ 2. Cupping is applicable to the Foot Taiyang Meridian at the first and second lines on the back.","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Xu Lao - Fatigue","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Quit Smoking Syndrome","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Baihui (GV20) and Shenmen (HT 7) calm the mind and relieve restless ness. Lieque (LU 7), close to the stop-smoking point, is a usefull point for the treatment of smokers who have quit the habit. Hegu (LI 4) and Lieque (LU 7) promote the function of the lungs in dissolving phlegm, strengthening the spleen and harmonizing the stomach. Danzhong (CV 17) is an influential point of qi and Neiguan (PC 6) connects with the heart and chest. These two can relax the chest and regulate qi for relieving the stagnation of qi in the chest. Zhaohai (KI 6) is a confluent point of the Kidney Meridian and the Yin Heel Vessel, Lieque (LU 7) is a confluent point of the Lung Meridian and the Conception Vessel, both connecting with the lung system and throat. They are indicated in cases of discomfort of the throat and dry throat with itching, Laogong (PC 8) and Tongli (HT 5) can clear the heart and calm the mind. Feishu (BL 13) is the Back-Shu point of the lungs, and can strengthen the lung qi.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Quit-smoking syndrome refers to a series of symptoms which are manifested after a heavy smoker quits smoking, including weakness of the whole body, restlessness, yawning, lack of taste, mental depression, fullness in the chest, anxiety and insensitivity. In ancient China there was no such ailment.","Patterns":"➣ Exogenous addictive materials such as nicotine in tobacco, after entering the body, combine with the opiate receptors of the central nervous system, inhibiting the secretion of endogenous opium materials and making the smoker dependent on the exogenous addictive substances. Once the intake of exogenous addictive materials stops, the secretion of endogenous opiates will not be able to meet the needs of the human body, thus inducing withdrawal symptoms.

➣ TCM holds that nicotine poison invades the body through the nose and mouth, damaging the spleen and lungs. At the same time, it invades the heart, so the mental functions rooted in the heart are disturbed.

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] A long history of smoking 10-20 cigarettes or more a day. Stopping smoking produces a strong desire to smoke. Symptoms such as listlessness, tiredness, anxiety, restlessness, yawning, tearing, salivation, lack of taste, discomfort in the throat, fullness in the chest, nausea and vomiting, muscular trembling and insensitivity result.

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Invasion by nicotine of the heart and brain is the cause of listlessness, anxiety and insensitivity. Because invasion by nicotine of the spleen and lungs causes spleen qi deficiency, there appears tiredness, yawning, tastelessness, nausea and vomiting, fullness in the chest and muscular trembling. When spleen qi is deficient in controlling the body fluid there will be tearing and salivation. Deficiency of lung qi and damage to the body fluid give rise to discomfort in the throat.","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"","Treatment Principle":"➣ [b]Method:[/b] Select the points mainly from the Lung Meridian of Hand Taiyin and the Heart Meridian of Hand Shaoyin for promoting the clearing of the lungs, dissolution of phlegm, calming of the mind and relief of restlessness. Apply acupuncture with a reducing or even method.","Point Prescription":"➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Baihui (GV20), Shenmen (HT 7), Lieque (LU 7), Hegu (LI 4).","Supplementary Points":"➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b] For fullness in the chest, gasping and profuse sputum, add Danzhong (CV 17) and Nei- guan (PC 6). For discomfort in the throat, add Zhaohai (KI 6). For irritability, add Laogong (PC 8) and Tongli (HT 5). For damage to lung qi, add Feishu (BL. 13).","Remarks":"➣ [b]Auriculotherapy for quit-smoking syndrome:[/b] Lungs, Mouth, Sympathetic Nerve, Shenmen, Subcortex. Puncture with filiform needle, or embed intradermal needles, or apply vaccaria seeds. Acupuncture, especially auricu- lotherapy, is very effective for treating quit smoking syndrome. For most patients with a desire to quit smoking, the expected effect will be achieved. Those who have embedded needles or seeds applied should press the points whenever a desire to smoke appears.","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Quit Smoking Syndrome","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Headache","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"[b]➣ a) Headache due to invasion of pathogenic wind into the meridians and collaterals[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Pain comes from obstruction of qi in the meridians and collaterals on the head caused by invasion of exogenous pathogenic wind. Owing to the excess of the pathogenic factor, the pain is violent and boring. Wind is a yang pathogenic factor and apt to attack the upper portion of the body. So the pain caused by wind may extend to the nape of the neck and back region. The fixed pain is due to blood stagnation derived from qi stagnation. String-taut pulse and thin white tongue coating are the signs of meridians and collaterals being invaded by pathogenic wind.

[b]➣ b) Headache due to upsurge of liver-yang[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Headache and blurred vision are due to rising of excessive liver-yang which attacks the head. Bitter taste in the mouth suggests accumulation of heat in the Gallbladder Meridian derived from the upsurge of liver- yang which affects the gallbladder, as the liver and gallbladder are externally and internally related. Severe pain on the bilateral sides of the

➣ head is because the Gallbladder Meridian travels bilaterally on the side of the head. String- taut and rapid pulse, red tongue with yellow coating are signs of heat in the gallbladder and liver.

[b]➣ c) Headache due to deficiency of both qi and blood[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Lingering headache is due to the head being affected by deficiency of qi that fails to make the clear yang ascend and the turbid yin descend. Pain aggravated by overstrain and stress is due to further consumption of qi. Lassitude and pain which is relieved by warmth and aggravated by cold suggest failure in distribution of yang qi. Lustreless face, dizziness and blurred vision indicate poor nourishment of the face and head due to deficiency of blood. Pale tongue with thin white coating and weak, thready pulse are signs of deficiency of both qi and blood.

➣ Clinically, varieties of headache should be also differentiated according to the locality and the related meridians and collaterals. Pain in the occipital region and nape of the neck is related to the Bladder Meridian of Foot- Taiyang, pain at the forehead and supraorbital region is related to the Stomach Meridian of Foot-Yangming, pain in bilateral or unilateral temporal region is related to the Gallbladder Meridian of Foot-Shaoyang, and that in the parietal region is related to the Liver Meridian of Foot-Jueyin.","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"[b]➣ a) Headache due to invasion of pathogenic wind into meridians and collaterals[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] The above prescriptions are formulated by combining local points with distal points according to the location of head- ache and the affected Meridian. Occipital headache - points of the Tai- yang Meridians of Hand and Foot. Frontal headache - points of the Yangming Meridians of Hand and Foot. Temporal headache - points of the Shaoyang Meridians of Hand and Foot. Parietal headache - points of the Taiyang Meridians of Hand and Foot plus those of the Jueyin Meridian of Foot.

[b]➣ b) Headache due to upsurge of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] The Jueyin Meridian of Foot reaches the parietal region and the Shaoyang Meridians run up to the bilateral sides of the head. Combining the local points with distal points can reduce heat in the meridians and pacify the liver yang.

[b]➣ c) Headache due to deficiency of both qi and blood:[/b]

➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Qihai (CV 6) is chosen to tonify the primary qi, and Baihui (GV 20) is for lifting up the clean yang. Ganshu (BL 18), Pishu (BL 20), and Shenshu (BL 23) are the points associated with the liver, spleen and kidney. Since the liver stores blood, the spleen controls blood, and the kidney stores and produces essence and blood, these three points can be used to strengthen essence in the kidney and to tonify qi and blood. Zusanli (ST 36), punctured with the reinforcing method, can benefit the stomach, which is the productive source of qi and blood.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Headache is a subjective symptom. It can be induced by various acute and chronic diseases. As it covers a wide sphere, this section only deals in detail with headache as the predominant symptom. If headache is an accompanying symptom in the development of a certain disease, it will disappear automatically as soon as the disease is cured. This type of headache is not discussed here.

➣ The head is the place where all the yang meridians of hand and foot meet, and qi and blood of the five zang organs and six fu organs all flow upward to the head. Attacks of endogenous or exogenous factors may cause headache due to derangement of qi and blood in the head and retardation of circulation of qi in the meridians that traverse the head. Headache caused by exogenous pathogenic factors is mostly due to invasion of pathogenic wind into the meridians and collaterals. [b]It is said:[/b] "When the pathogenic wind invades the human body, it first attacks the upper portion of the body." Headache caused by endogenous factors often originates from hyperfunction of the liver yang, or deficiency of both qi and blood.","Patterns":"➣[b] a) Invasion of pathogenic wind into the upper meridians and collaterals causes derangement and obstruction of qi and blood. With stagnation in the collaterals, sudden weather change or exposure to wind usually precipitates an attack of headache.[/b]

➣[b] b) In patients with excessive yang of body constitution, headache may be caused by upsurge of liver yang due to stagnation of qi or injury of the liver after a fit of anger, which damages the yin.[/b]

➣[b] c) Headache may also be caused by deficiency of both qi and blood because of irregular food intake, overstrain and stress, poor health with a chronic disease, or congenital deficiency.[/b]

➣ Deficiency of qi prevents the clear yang from ascending, and deficiency of blood does not nourish the mind, so there is headache.","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"[b]➣ a) Headache due to invasion of pathogenic wind into the meridians and collaterals[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Headache occurs on exposure to wind. The pain may extend to the nape of the neck and back regions. It is a violent, boring and fixed pain, accompanied by string-taut pulse and thin white tongue coating. Such a syndrome is also termed "head wind."

[b]➣ b) Headache due to upsurge of liver-yang[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Headache, blurred vision, severe pain on the bilateral sides of the head, irritability, hot temper, flushed face, bitter taste in the mouth, string-taut and rapid pulse, reddened tongue with yellow coating.

➣ head is because the Gallbladder Meridian travels bilaterally on the side of the head. String- taut and rapid pulse, red tongue with yellow coating are signs of heat in the gallbladder and liver.

[b]➣ c) Headache due to deficiency of both qi and blood[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Lingering headache, dizziness, blurred vision, lassitude, lustreless face, pain relieved by warmth and aggravated by cold, overstrain or mental stress, weak and thready pulse, tongue with thin and white coat- ing.

➣ Clinically, varieties of headache should be also differentiated according to the locality and the related meridians and collaterals. Pain in the occipital region and nape of the neck is related to the Bladder Meridian of Foot- Taiyang, pain at the forehead and supraorbital region is related to the Stomach Meridian of Foot-Yangming, pain in bilateral or unilateral temporal region is related to the Gallbladder Meridian of Foot-Shaoyang, and that in the parietal region is related to the Liver Meridian of Foot-Jueyin.","Treatment Principle":"[b]➣ a) Headache due to invasion of pathogenic wind into meridians and collaterals[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] To dispel the wind, remove obstruction in the meridians and collaterals, regulate the qi and blood and reduce the pain by puncturing the local points combined with distal points along the related meridians. The reducing method with needle retention is used.

[b]➣ b) Headache due to upsurge of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Select points of Jueyin and Shao- yang Meridians of Hand and Foot as the principal points to pacify the liver yang. Puncture with the reducing method.

[b]➣ c) Headache due to deficiency of both qi and blood:[/b]

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Tonify and regulate circulation of qi and blood, promote the clean qi to ascend and the turbid qi to descend, by needling points of the Governor and Conception vessels and the corresponding Back-Shu points. Puncture with the reinforcing method.","Point Prescription":"➣ [b] a) Headache due to invasion of pathogenic wind into meridians and collaterals[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b]

➣ [b]Occipital headache:[/b] Fengchi (GB 20), Kunlun (BL 60), Houxi (SI 3).

➣ [b]Frontal, headache:[/b] Touwei (ST 8), Yintang (GV 29), Shangxing (GV 23), Hegu (LI 4), Neiting (ST 44).

➣ [b]Temporal headache:[/b] Taiyang (Extra), Shuai- gu (GB 8), Waiguan (TE 5), Zulinqi(GB 41).

➣ [b]Parietal headache:[/b] Baihui (GV 20), Houxi (SI 3), Zhiyin (BL 67), Taichong (LR 3).

➣ [b]b) Headache due to upsurge of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Fengchi (GB 20), Baihui (GV 20), Xuanlu (GB 5), Xiaxi (GB 43), Xingjian (LR 2).

➣ [b]c) Headache due to deficiency of both qi and blood:[/b]

➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Baihui (GV 20), Qihai (CV 6), Ganshu (BL 18), Pishu (BL 20), Shenshu (BL 23), Zusanli (ST 36).","Supplementary Points":"➣ [b] a) Headache due to invasion of pathogenic wind into meridians and collaterals[/b]

➣ [b] b) Headache due to upsurge of liver yang[/b]

➣ [b] c) Headache due to deficiency of both qi and blood:[/b]","Remarks":"➣ Headache occurs in various diseases of modern internal medicine, surgery, neurology, psychosis, ear, nose, throat, etc. Acupuncture gives gratifying results in migraine, and in vascular and neurotic headache.

➣ [b]Dermal needle and cupping therapies:[/b]

➣ [b]Main points:[/b] Area along L 1 to S 4 of the spine.

➣ [b]Secondary points:[/b] Fengchi (GB 20), Tai- yang (Extra), Yangbai (GB 14).

➣ [b]Method:[/b] Tap on the area from L 1 to S 4 of the spine. Then tap on the local area and along the afflicted meridians. For acute pain, Taiyang (Extra) and Yangbai (GB 14) may be tapped to slight bleeding, then apply cupping,","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Headache","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Facial Pain","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"[b]➣ a) Facial pain due to invasion by pathogenic wind and cold[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Pain is caused by the obstruction circulation of qi and blood in the meridians and collaterals on the face due to invasion of pathogenic wind and cold. Pain aggravated by pressure suggests that the pathogenic factors are in excess. Burning pain comes from the fierce fight between the anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors. String-taut and tense pulse is the sign of invasion by pathogenic wind cold. Endogenous wind heat comes from prolonged accumulation of exogenous pathogenic cold wind, giving rise to spasm, running nose, tearing and salivation.

[b]➣ b) Facial pain due to excessive fire in the liver and stomach[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Irritability and hot temper are due to fire caused by prolonged depression of the liver qi. Burning pain is caused by endogenous heat coming from prolonged retention of food in the stomach, which rises to the face through the Stomach Meridian. Thirst and constipation are due to heat in the stomach. Yellow, dry tongue coating, string-taut and rapid pulse are signs of accumulation of fire in the liver and stomach.

[b]➣ c) Facial pain due to deficiency of yin and excessive fire[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] The kidney stores essence and dominates water. When the kidney essence is insufficient, lassitude, soreness in the lumbar region and emaciation occur. Insufficiency of kidney water fails in controlling fire, which flares up along the meridians and reaches the face, causing malar flush and facial pain. Thready, rapid pulse, red tongue with little coating are signs of deficiency of yin with flaming fire.","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] The above prescriptions are formulated by combining the local points with the distal points according to the location of pain and the meridians affected. For instance, Xiaguan (ST 7), Jiache (ST 6) and Jiachengjiang (Extra) are the points located at the mandibu- lar region. Hegu (LI 4) and Waiguan (TE 5) are the points along the Hand-Yangming and Hand-Shaoyang Meridians which go upward to the facial region. The above prescription has the effect of promoting circulation of qi of the meridians and collaterals in the affected area, and the function of reducing excess and relieving pain. Fengchi (GB 20), the meeting point of the Foot-Shaoyang Meridian and Yang Link Vessel, can be used to dispel wind and to stop pain. Taichong (LR 3) and Neiting (ST 44) can be chosen for the purpose of eliminating excessive fire in the liver and stomach. Zhaohai (KI 6) and Sanyinjiao (SP 6) nourish the yin and reduce the fire. For a chronic disease, local points on the affected side can be punctured with the reinforcing method, shallow insertion and needle retention. Or the reducing method with long retaining of needles can be applied to the corresponding local points of the healthy side.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Facial pain is a kind of severe pain occurring in transient paroxysms in a certain facial region. It mostly occurs in one side of the forehead, maxillary region or mandibular region. The onset is abrupt like an electric shock, and the pain is cutting, burning and intolerable. Frequent recurrence denotes a chronic disease. In most cases it starts after middle age in women.","Patterns":"➣ A sudden attack of this disease is due to invasion of the meridians and collaterals on the face by pathogenic wind cold which contracts the meridians and collaterals and retards the circulation of qi and blood. [b]In the thirty- eighth chapter of Plain Questions, it says:[/b] "When pathogenic cold comes and stays in the meridians it impedes and slows down the circulation. If it lodges outside the vessels, the blood supply is decreased, and if it remains in the vessels, the passage of qi is obstructed, resulting in a sudden attack of pain."

➣ Facial pain may also arise from excessive fire of the liver and stomach, which flares up and attacks the face. The fire in the stomach is produced by retention of food caused by irregular food intake. The fire of the liver is due to the stagnation of qi in the liver. Furthermore, facial pain may be due to deficiency of yin producing excess of fire in patients with a body constitution of yin deficiency and excessive sexual activity whichs consumes essence. In addition, diseases of the teeth, mouth, ear, nose, or mental disorders may also induce facial pain.","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"[b]➣ a) Facial pain due to invasion by pathogenic wind and cold[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Abrupt onset of pain occurs like an electric shock. The pain is cut- ting, boring and intolerable, but transient and paroxysmal. Each attack lasts a few seconds or one to two minutes. It may recur several times a day. Tender points can be found on the su- praobital foramen, infraorbital foramen, cheek foramen, lateral side of ala nasi, angle of the mouth, and nasolabial groove, where pressure induces the attack of pain. The pain is often accompanied by local spasm, running nose and tearing, salivation, or by exterior symptoms with string-taut and tense pulse.

[b]➣ b) Facial pain due to excessive fire in the liver and stomach[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] The attack of pain as described above is accompanied by irritability, hot temper, thirst, constipation, yellow and dry tongue coating, and string-taut, rapid pulse.

[b]➣ c) Facial pain due to deficiency of yin and excessive fire[/b]

➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Insidious pain, emaciation, malar flush, soreness in the lumbar region, lassitude, pain aggravated by fatigue, thready and rapid pulse, red tongue with little coating,","Treatment Principle":"➣ [b]Method:[/b] Select the local points in combination with distal points according to the location of pain and the meridians affected. For facial pain due to invasion of pathogenic wind and cold, reducing method is used to promote the circulation of qi and blood in the diseased area. For facial pain due to excessive fire in the liver and stomach, the points along the Foot- Jueyin and Yangming Meridians are punctured with the reducing method to bring down the fire. For facial pain due to deficiency of yin and excessive fire, the points along the Foot- Shaoyin Meridian should be added and punctured with the reinforcing method to nourish the yin and to dissipate the fire.","Point Prescription":"➣ [b]Prescription:[/b]

➣ [b]Pain at supraobital Region:[/b] Yangbai (GB 14), Taiyang (Extra), Cuanzhu (BL 2), Waiguan (TE 5).

➣ [b]Pain at maxillary region:[/b] Sibai (ST 2), Quanliao (SI 18), Yingxiang (LI 20), Hegu (LI 4).

➣ [b]Pain at mandibular region:[/b] Xiaguan (ST 7), Jiache (ST 6), Daying (ST 5), Jiachengjiang (Extra), Hegu (LI 4).","Supplementary Points":"➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b]

➣ [b]Invasion by pathogenic wind and cold:[/b] Fengchi (GB 20).

➣ [b]Excessive fire in the liver and stomach:[/b] Taichong (LR 3), Neiting (ST 44).

➣ [b]Deficiency of yin and excessive fire:[/b] Zhao- hai (KI 6), Sanyinjiao (SP 6).","Remarks":"[b]➣ a) Facial pain is referred to as trigeminal neuralgia in modern medicine.[/b]

[b]➣ b) Acupuncture is effective in pain of primary trigeminal neuralgia. For secondary trigeminal neuralgia accompanying intracranial diseases or lesions of the nervous system, in which the pain is usually continuous with paroxysms of aggravation, treatment should be aimed at its primary cause.[/b]","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Facial Pain","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Deviation of Eye and Mouth","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] It is known that the Foot and Hand Yangming and Shaoyang Meridians supply the facial region, and the Muscle Meridians of Hand and Foot Yangming and Shaoyang also reach the forehead, cheek, and the front of the ear. The above manifestations of devia- tion of the eye and mouth are due to flaccidity of the affected muscles leading to imbalance of facial muscles between the two sides. The paralytic muscles are caused by stagnation of qi in the meridians and malnutrition of the regions of the muscle meridians after the invasion of pathogenic wind and cold.","Treatment Method":"","Prescription":"","Explanation":"➣ [b]Explanation:[/b] Hegu (LI 4), the Yuan- Primary) point of the Large Intestine Meridian of Hand-Yangming, can eliminate pathogenic wind from the head and facial region. Wangu (GB 12) and Tinghui (GB 2) can eliminate wind and relieve headache. Yangbai (GB 14), Taiyang (Extra), Cuanzhu (BL 2), Sizhukong (TE 23), Tongziliao (GB 1), Yuyao (Extra), Ju- liao (ST 3), Renzhong (GV 26), Dicang (ST 4), Jiache (ST 6), Yingxiang (LI 20) and Quanliao (TE 17) are all local points of the involved meridians and have the effect of eliminating wind and invigorating circulation of meridians.","Supplementary & Remarks":"","Combinations":"","Description":"➣ Deviation of the eye and the mouth is derived from invasion of the meridians and collaterals and muscle meridians in the facial region by exogeneous pathogenic wind and cold. It can occur in patients of any age, but mostly at the age of twenty to forty, and more frequently in males.","Patterns":"➣ Deviation of the eye and the mouth is due to paralysis of the facial muscles caused by the attack of pathogenic wind and cold on Yang- ming and Shaoyang Meridians, which leads to malnutrition of the muscle regions of the meridians.","Diagnosis & Differentiation":"➣ [b]Main manifestations:[/b] Sudden onset, usually right after waking up, incomplete closure of the eye in the affected side, drooping of the angle of the mouth, salivation and inability to frown, raise the eyebrow, close the eye, blow out the cheek, show the teeth or whistle, and in some cases pain in the mastoid region or headache, thin, white tongue coating, superficial tense or superficial slow pulse.","Treatment Principle":"➣ [b]Method:[/b] To eliminate wind and remove the obstruction of meridians by applying even- movement mainly to the points of Hand and Foot Yangming Meridians, and also to the points of Shaoyang Meridians.","Point Prescription":"➣ [b]Prescription:[/b] Yifeng(TE 17), Yangbai (GB 14), Taiyang (Extra), Quanliao (SI 18), Xiaguan (ST 7), Dicang (ST 4), Jiache (ST 6), Hegu (LI 4).","Supplementary Points":"➣ [b]Supplementary points:[/b]

➣ [b]Headache:[/b] Fengchi (GB 20).

➣ [b]Difficulty in frowning and raising the eyebrow:[/b] Zhanzhu (BL 2), Sizhukong (TE 23).

➣ [b]Incomplete closing of the eye:[/b] Cuanzhu (BL 2), Jingming (BL 1), Tongziliao (GB I), Yuyao (Extra), Sizhukong (TE 23).

➣ [b]Difficulty in sniffing:[/b] Yingxiang (LI 20).

➣ [b]Deviation of the philtrum:[/b] Renzhong (GV 26).

➣ [b]Inability to show the teeth:[/b] Juliao (ST 3).

➣ [b]Tinnitus and deafness:[/b] Tinghui (GB 2).

➣ [b]Tenderness at the mastoid region:[/b] Wangu (GB 12), Waiguan (TE 5).","Remarks":"[b]➣ a) This condition is seen in peripheral facial paralysis or Bell's palsy in modern medicine.[/b]

[b]➣ b) In long-standing cases, warming needle or moxibustion may be used on the points Taiyang (Extra), Jiache (ST 6), Dicang (ST 4), Juliao (ST 3), and Xiaguan (ST 7).[/b]

[b]➣ c) Cupping therapy: Cupping may be used as an adjuvant method to acupuncture. The affected side may be treated with small cups once every three to five days.[/b]

[b]➣ d) If the healthy side of the face is stiff, shallow puncture and needle retention at the local points of the healthy side can be applied in combination with needling of the affected side.[/b]","Flashcard View":"","Notes":"","CAM Notes":"","Important":"","Channel Full Name":"Zang-Fu Syndromes","Channel Short Name":"Deviation of Eye and Mouth","Image ID":""},{"Short Name":"Neck Pain","Full Name":"StudyGuide","Main Topic & Etiology":"","Subtopic & Manifestations":"","Analysis":"[b]➣ a) Wind cold obstruction[/b]

➣ [b]Analysis:[/b] Wind-cold-damp obstructs the meridians, and qi and blood circulation is blocked. The obstruction causes pain. Cold is stagnant in nature, and therefore the symptoms will be aggravated when exposed to cold. Thin, white or white sticky tongue coating and string-taut and tense pulse are signs of invasion of wind cold.

[b]➣ b) Blood stasis due to overexertion[/b]
